children.heading: Adverse event
children.label: 1 Adverse event
children.text: The term “adverse event” means any health-related event associated with the use of a cosmetic product that is adverse.

children.heading: Cosmetic product
children.label: 2 Cosmetic product
children.text: The term “cosmetic product” means a preparation of cosmetic ingredients with a qualitatively and quantitatively set composition for use in a finished product.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term “facility” includes any establishment (including an establishment of an importer) that manufactures or processes cosmetic products distributed in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: Beauty shops and salons, unless such establishment manufactures or processes cosmetic products at that location.

children.label: ii
children.text: Cosmetic product retailers, including individual sales representatives, direct sellers (as defined in ), retail distribution facilities, and pharmacies, unless such establishment manufactures or processes cosmetic products that are not sold directly to consumers at that location.section 3508(b)(2) of title 26

children.label: iii
children.text: Hospitals, physicians’ offices, and health care clinics.

children.label: iv
children.text: Public health agencies and other nonprofit entities that provide cosmetic products directly to the consumer.

children.label: v
children.text: Entities (such as hotels and airlines) that provide complimentary cosmetic products to customers incidental to other services.

children.label: vi
children.text: Trade shows and other venues where cosmetic product samples are provided free of charge.

children.label: vii
children.text: An establishment that manufactures or processes cosmetic products that are solely for use in research or evaluation, including for production testing and not offered for retail sale.

children.label: I
children.text: Labeling.

children.label: II
children.text: Relabeling.

children.label: III
children.text: Packaging.

children.label: IV
children.text: Repackaging.

children.label: V
children.text: Holding.

children.label: VI
children.text: Distributing.

children.heading: .—Clarification
children.label: C .—Clarification
children.text: For the purposes of subparagraph (B)(viii), the terms “packaging” and “repackaging” do not include filling a product container with a cosmetic product.

children.heading: Responsible person
children.label: 4 Responsible person
children.text: The term “responsible person” means the manufacturer, packer, or distributor of a cosmetic product whose name appears on the label of such cosmetic product in accordance with or .section 364e(a) of this titlesection 1453(a) of title 15

children.label: i
children.text: death;

children.label: ii
children.text: a life-threatening experience;

children.label: iii
children.text: inpatient hospitalization;

children.label: iv
children.text: a persistent or significant disability or incapacity;

children.label: v
children.text: a congenital anomaly or birth defect;

children.label: vi
children.text: an infection; or

children.label: vii
children.text: significant disfigurement (including serious and persistent rashes, second- or third-degree burns, significant hair loss, or persistent or significant alteration of appearance), other than as intended, under conditions of use that are customary or usual; or

children.label: B
children.text: requires, based on reasonable medical judgment, a medical or surgical intervention to prevent an outcome described in subparagraph (A).
